prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in the third quarter of this strange fiscal year, the Lords of Software did amass a bounty of coin, though it flowed not from the sale of silks or spices, but from invisible scrolls delivered through the ether. The scribes report that a thousand souls did subscribe to the enchanted service, which doth run perpetually on devices that glow like the fires of a thousand dragons, yet emit no smoke. Profit, like unto a mysterious beast, did leap forth from the ledger, confounding the accountants who wear spectacles and converse in riddles of “click-through rates” and “churn.” And lo, the chief wizard of this enterprise proclaimed that their cloud, though unseen and weightless as a summer breeze, held more power than the strongest catapults of yore, enabling the kingdom’s subjects to conjure their tasks with but a flick of a finger.
